entries.title: Mandalorian Wars Pressure the Republic Border

content.fact: The Mandalorian Wars begin as a brutal frontier conflict that the Republic struggles to answer quickly.
content.injection: During Mandalorian Wars scenes, show the Republic as politically hesitant while frontier worlds suffer immediate military pressure.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Mandalorian Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Mandalorian Wars Begin to Malachor V Ends the Mandalorian Wars.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

content.fact: The Jedi Council hesitates to enter the Mandalorian Wars, creating a moral and generational split inside the Order.
content.injection: Before Revanite intervention, write many Jedi as restrained or conflicted; interventionist Jedi view inaction as betrayal of worlds under attack.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Mandalorian Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Mandalorian Wars Begin to Revanite Jedi Intervene.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

content.fact: Revan’s Jedi followers defy Council caution and become wartime commanders against the Mandalorians.
content.injection: During Revanite intervention, treat these Jedi as heroic to many soldiers but politically and spiritually alarming to the Council.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Mandalorian Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Revanite Jedi Intervene to Malachor V Ends the Mandalorian Wars.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

content.fact: Malachor V ends the Mandalorian Wars through catastrophic destruction that traumatizes survivors and wounds the Force.
content.injection: At or after Malachor V, write victory as morally contaminated: the battle breaks fleets, armies, Mandalorian power, and Force-sensitive survivors.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Mandalorian Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Malachor V Ends the Mandalorian Wars to Malachor V Ends the Mandalorian Wars.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

content.fact: After Malachor V, Mandalorian military power is shattered but Mandalorian culture and scattered warriors persist.
content.injection: Post-Malachor, treat Mandalorians as defeated, leaderless, and dispersed rather than extinct or irrelevant. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Malachor V Ends the Mandalorian Wars to Star Forge Destroyed.

entries.title: Revan and Malak Return as Sith

content.fact: Revan and Malak return from the Unknown Regions as Sith conquerors, turning former Republic heroes into the Republic’s greatest crisis.
content.injection: During the Jedi Civil War, write Revan and Malak’s threat as militarily credible because they know Republic systems, officers, tactics, and Jedi vulnerabilities.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Jedi Civil War.
Respect story-position bounds from Jedi Civil War Begins to Jedi Civil War Begins.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

entries.title: Star Forge Produces Sith War Machine

content.fact: The Star Forge enables the Sith war effort by producing vast fleets and material through ancient Rakatan technology.
content.injection: When the Star Forge is active, the Sith threat can replenish ships and pressure the Republic beyond normal industrial limits.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Jedi Civil War Begins to Star Forge Destroyed.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

entries.title: Bastila Battle Meditation Is Strategic Asset

content.fact: Bastila Shan’s Battle Meditation is a strategic Force asset that can sway fleet engagements and morale.
content.injection: In KOTOR-era scenes, treat Bastila’s Battle Meditation as a military objective, not just a personal Jedi talent.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR Era.

entries.title: Revan Identity Is Gated KOTOR Spoiler

content.fact: Revan’s player-era identity and memory state are spoiler-sensitive during KOTOR context.
content.injection: Before the Revan reveal is established in the story, do not casually reveal Revan’s identity, memory reconstruction, or prior Sith command status.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Star Forge Campaign to Star Forge Destroyed.
antiLore: Do not spoil the Revan identity twist unless the active Context is after that reveal or the user’s story introduced it.
Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Star Forge Destroyed Ends Malak Crisis

content.fact: The destruction of the Star Forge and Malak’s defeat end the main Jedi Civil War crisis.
content.injection: After the Star Forge’s destruction, do not keep Malak’s Sith war machine at full strength unless the story branches deliberately.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR Era.

content.fact: The Republic survives the Jedi Civil War but is politically, militarily, and spiritually damaged.
content.injection: After KOTOR, write the Republic as surviving but depleted; easy stability should not snap back immediately. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Dark Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Star Forge Destroyed to Dark Wars Begin.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Sith Triumvirate Operates as Hidden Predator

content.fact: The Sith Triumvirate hunts Jedi from the shadows rather than ruling as an open galactic empire.
content.injection: During KOTOR II/Dark Wars context, write the Sith threat as hidden, predatory, and metaphysical: Jedi vanish, survivors hide, and Force wounds matter.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Dark Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Dark Wars Begin to Malachor V Finale.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Meetra Surik Exile Is Force Wound

content.fact: Meetra Surik’s exile is defined by Malachor trauma and a severed or wounded relationship to the Force.
content.injection: For KOTOR II scenes, write Meetra as shaped by survivor guilt, broken Force connection, and the consequences of command at Malachor.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Dark Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Dark Wars Begin to Malachor V Finale.

entries.title: Nihilus Is Hunger, Not Normal Sith Lord

content.fact: Darth Nihilus is best treated as a consuming wound and hunger in the Force rather than a conventional political Sith ruler.
content.injection: When Nihilus is relevant, write him as an existential Force catastrophe whose presence threatens life and worlds, not as a normal scheming warlord.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Dark Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Dark Wars Begin to Malachor V Finale.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Kreia Frames the Force as Problem

content.fact: Kreia’s role is philosophical and manipulative: she challenges Jedi/Sith binaries and treats dependence on the Force as the central problem.
content.injection: In KOTOR II scenes, write Kreia as testing motives, attacking certainty, and manipulating students toward her argument about the Force itself.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Dark Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Dark Wars Begin to Malachor V Finale.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

entries.title: Malachor Finale Ends Triumvirate Immediate Threat

content.fact: The Malachor finale resolves the immediate Sith Triumvirate crisis and opens space for Jedi restoration.
content.injection: After the Malachor finale, do not keep the Triumvirate as an active intact organization unless the story intentionally diverges.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Dark Wars.
